preamble: The transformation of the alveolar trill [r] into the diphthong [ai] is a notable phonetic shift, primarily occurring through processes of vocalization. R-vocalization involves the loss of the consonantal quality of /r/, often in syllable-coda positions, leading to its realization as a vowel or as part of a diphthong. This can happen either as a direct development of a vocalic /r/ or through the influence of a vocalized /r/ on an adjacent vowel, resulting in the formation of the [ai] diphthong.
phoneticEffects: Vocalization, Diphthongization, Rhotacization, Vowel Gradation, Sandhi

examples.note: Vocalization of coda /r/ after a long /a:/, resulting in the rhotacized diphthong [ai] in certain contexts.

sources: Macdonell, A. A. (1916). A Vedic Grammar for Students. Oxford University Press.
